mission.title: clickhouse: Support of dynamic subcolumns in tables.
mission.description: Support of dynamic subcolumns in tables.. Requested by 23+ users.

steps.title: Check current clickhouse setup
steps.description:
Verify your clickhouse version and configuration:
```bash
clickhouse-client --version
```
This feature requires a working clickhouse installation.

steps.title: Review clickhouse configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant clickhouse configuration:
Inroduce new data type `Object(<schema format>)`, which will get the name of format for semi-structured data (`JSON`, `XML`, etc.).
Initially it will work only with `MergeTree` tables. Maybe later will add some other storages.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support of dynamic subcolumns in tables.
Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Support of dynamic subcolumns in tables." is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Resolved via PR #23932 which added the new `Object('JSON')` data type for dynamic subcolumns. This allows storing semi-structured JSON data where all paths are automatically extracted and stored as separate columns in their most optimal types. Subcolumns can be accessed by dot-notation path names (e.g., `data.key1.key2`) and optionally cast to specific types using the `::` operator. Initially supported with MergeTree tables.

prerequisites.description: A working clickhouse installation or development environment.
